---
title: "CV"
---

## Current position

**Maître Assistant** (Associate Professor), IMT Mines Albi, Centre Génie Industriel\
Enseignante-chercheuse, axe Territoire (TRACE) et Gestion des Opérations Logistiques (GOL)\
2021 – Present

<!-- PLACEHOLDER / À VALIDER: this CV's most recent publications (2023-2026) are heavily Physical Internet / rural mobility, but this document doesn't mention that shift. Confirm your title is still accurate and current before publishing. -->

## Education

::: {.timeline-entry}
**PhD, Génie Industriel et Informatique**\
Université de Toulouse, IMT Mines Albi, Centre Génie Industriel\
2017 – 2020\
Thesis: "Une démarche outillée de diagnostic organisationnel et d'amélioration à base de double numérique : Application aux centres d'appels de trois SAMU"\
Supervisors: Matthieu Lauras, Franck Fontanili (IMT Mines Albi)\
**Prix de la meilleure thèse 2020**, GdR MACS and the Automatique section of Club EEA
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
**Maîtrise Recherche ès Science**, Industrial Engineering and Applied Mathematics\
École Polytechnique de Montréal, Canada\
2015 – 2017\
Research project: optimization of inter-hospital non-emergency patient transport\
Supervisors: Prof. L.-M. Rousseau, Prof. N. Lahrichi (Polytechnique Montréal, Chaire HANALOG, GERAD, CIRRELT)
:::

::: {.timeline-entry}
**Élève Ingénieur**, Génie Industriel, Processus et Systèmes d'Informations\
IMT Mines Albi\
2013 – 2016
:::

## Postdoctoral project

**CallSamu** (2021): developing collaboration between SAMU emergency call centers at the regional scale (Pays de la Loire)\
Partner institutions: IMT Mines Albi, CHU de Nantes, SAMU 44, SAMU 85, SAMU 53, SAMU 72\
Keywords: emergency call centers, collaboration, modeling, discrete event simulation, process mining, data mining, model transformation, hospital systems




## Networks and affiliations

<!-- - IPIC, EULiST, ANR, ADEME, ALICE ETP <!-- from prior context, confirm -->
<!--- SAGIP (Société d'Automatique de Génie Industriel et de Productique)
- GISEH (Gestion et Ingénierie des Systèmes Hospitaliers)



## Teaching

2017 – 2020: 158 HETD, undergraduate and graduate level (L3, M1, M2), Génie Industriel\
Courses: Gestion de Projet, Gestion de Production, Analyse des Systèmes, Modélisation de processus et simulation de flux à événements discrets, Optimisation Discrète, Outils Informatiques\
Typical group size: 25 to 30 students per TD/CM group

## Supervision

- M1 internship, 2019 (4 months): packaging supply chain at a Chinese manufacturing company
- M1 internship, 2020 (3 months): forecasting emergency department patient arrivals

## Other professional experience

**Assistant Engineer**, DRH, CHU de Toulouse, April – August 2015\
Process formalization for ISO 9001 certification, deployment of a Talentsoft recruitment/mobility tool, compilation of the 2014 Bilan Social, field visits in cardiology departments to identify IT needs.

## Languages and tools

English (C1), German (B2), French (native)\
Programming: Python, Java, C++\
Scientific tools: Matlab, NumPy, SciPy, R, Witness, FlexSim\
LaTeX

## Science communication

- 2018: Ma Thèse en 180 secondes, regional final, Université de Toulouse
- 2017–2019: Nuit Européenne des Chercheurs (Albi and Toulouse), participant and organizer
- 2019: Ma Thèse sur Un Mur, collaboration between researchers and an artist
- 2018–2020: Pint of Science, organizer (2018, Toulouse) and local coordinator (2019, Albi)
- 2019–present: Comédienne, Science Comedy Show, writing and performing science-themed sketches and street theatre
- 2020–2021: Co-founder and admin of a 1200-member francophone PhD student community (Discord), formed against isolation during the Covid-19 pandemic

## Research interests beyond current focus

Simulation methods, Operations Research, Hospital systems, Humanitarian logistics

## Personal interests

Théâtre, sewing, Lindy Hop, climbing
